examples.label: Console Query Example - SELECT with WHERE AND
examples.sql:
SELECT *
FROM Music
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='Happy Day'

examples.label: NoSQL Workbench parameterized SELECT
examples.sql:
SELECT *
FROM Music
WHERE Artist=? and SongTitle=?

examples.label: INSERT statement with tuple value
Basic SELECT statement
examples.sql:
SELECT * FROM Music
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with two SET clauses
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
SET AwardsWon=1
SET AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]}
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with list_append function
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
SET AwardDetail.Grammys =list_append(AwardDetail.Grammys,[2016])
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with REMOVE list element
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
REMOVE AwardDetail.Grammys[2]
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE adding new map member
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
SET AwardDetail.BillBoard=[2020]
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with bag/string set literal
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
SET BandMembers =<<'member1', 'member2'>>
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with set_add function
examples.sql:
UPDATE Music
SET BandMembers =set_add(BandMembers, <<'newmember'>>)
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: DELETE statement
examples.sql:
DELETE
FROM Music
WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: Parameterized SELECT with quoted table name
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Music" WHERE Artist=? AND SongTitle=?

examples.label: Parameterized SELECT (unquoted table name)
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM Music WHERE Artist=? AND SongTitle=?

examples.label: INSERT exercising all data types (map, list, number set, string set)
examples.sql: INSERT INTO TypesTable value {'primarykey':'1',  'NumberType':1, 'MapType' : {'entryname1': 'value', 'entryname2': 4},  'ListType': [1,'stringval'],  'NumberSetType':<<1,34,32,4.5>>,  'StringSetType':<<'stringval','stringval2'>> }

examples.label: UPDATE adding elements across all data types
examples.sql: UPDATE TypesTable  SET NumberType=NumberType + 100  SET MapType.NewMapEntry=[2020, 'stringvalue', 2.4] SET ListType = LIST_APPEND(ListType, [4, <<'string1', 'string2'>>]) SET NumberSetType= SET_ADD(NumberSetType, <<345, 48.4>>) SET StringSetType = SET_ADD(StringSetType, <<'stringsetvalue1', 'stringsetvalue2'>>) WHERE primarykey='1'

examples.label: UPDATE removing elements across all data types
examples.sql: UPDATE TypesTable  SET NumberType=NumberType - 1 REMOVE ListType[1] REMOVE MapType.NewMapEntry SET NumberSetType = SET_DELETE( NumberSetType, <<345>>) SET StringSetType = SET_DELETE( StringSetType, <<'stringsetvalue1'>>) WHERE primarykey='1'

examples.label: SELECT syntax skeleton
examples.sql: SELECT `expression`  [, ...]  FROM `table`[.`index`] [ WHERE `condition` ] [ [ORDER BY `key` [DESC|ASC] , ...]

examples.label: SELECT * with partition key equality
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID = 100

examples.label: SELECT * partition key + non-key AND
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID = 100 and Address='some address'

examples.label: SELECT * partition key OR partition key
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID = 100 or OrderID = 200

examples.label: SELECT * with IN list
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID IN [100, 300, 234]

examples.label: SELECT * with greater-than condition
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID > 1

examples.label: SELECT * with non-key WHERE
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE Address='some address'

examples.label: SELECT * with OR across key and non-key
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "Orders"  WHERE OrderID = 100 OR Address='some address'

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with key equality
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total FROM "Orders" WHERE OrderID = 1

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with OR
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total FROM "Orders" WHERE OrderID = 1 OR OrderID = 2

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with IN and ORDER BY DESC
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total FROM "Orders" WHERE OrderID IN [1, 2, 3] ORDER BY OrderID DESC

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with greater-than non-key
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total  FROM "Orders" WHERE Total > 500

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with IN literal list
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total  FROM "Orders" WHERE Total IN [500, 600]

examples.label: SELECT projected columns with BETWEEN
examples.sql: SELECT OrderID, Total  FROM "Orders"  WHERE Total BETWEEN 500 AND 600

examples.label: SELECT using nested document path
examples.sql: SELECT Devices.FireStick.DateWatched[0]  FROM WatchList  WHERE CustomerID= 'C1' AND MovieID= 'M1'

examples.label: SELECT with WHERE on nested document path
examples.sql: SELECT Devices  FROM WatchList  WHERE Devices.FireStick.DateWatched[0] >= '12/24/19'

examples.label: SELECT querying a secondary index
examples.sql: SELECT *  FROM "TableName"."IndexName"

examples.label: INSERT with quoted table name
examples.sql: INSERT INTO "Music" value {'Artist' : 'Acme Band','SongTitle' : 'PartiQL Rocks'}

examples.label: INSERT syntax skeleton
examples.sql: INSERT INTO `table` VALUE `item`

examples.label: UPDATE with two SET clauses (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET AwardsWon=1  SET AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]}   WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with RETURNING ALL OLD *
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET AwardsWon=1  SET AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]}   WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks' RETURNING ALL OLD *

examples.label: UPDATE with RETURNING ALL NEW *
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET AwardsWon=1  SET AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]}   WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks' RETURNING ALL NEW *

examples.label: UPDATE with list_append function (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET AwardDetail.Grammys =list_append(AwardDetail.Grammys,[2016])   WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE REMOVE list element (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  REMOVE AwardDetail.Grammys[2]    WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE adding map member (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET AwardDetail.BillBoard=[2020]  WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with bag/string set literal (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET BandMembers =<<'member1', 'member2'>>  WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: UPDATE with set_add function (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music"  SET BandMembers =set_add(BandMembers, <<'newbandmember'>>)  WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: Basic DELETE with quoted identifiers
examples.sql: DELETE FROM "Music" WHERE "Artist" = 'Acme Band' AND "SongTitle" = 'PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: DELETE with RETURNING ALL OLD *
examples.sql: DELETE FROM "Music" WHERE "Artist" = 'Acme Band' AND "SongTitle" = 'PartiQL Rocks' RETURNING ALL OLD *

examples.label: SELECT using SIZE function
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Orders" WHERE "OrderID"=1 AND size("Image") >300

examples.label: EXISTS function wrapping a SELECT
examples.sql: EXISTS(     SELECT * FROM "Music"      WHERE "Artist" = 'Acme Band' AND "SongTitle" = 'PartiQL Rocks')

examples.label: SELECT using attribute_type function
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Music" WHERE attribute_type("Artist", 'S')

examples.label: SELECT using begins_with function
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Orders" WHERE "OrderID"=1 AND begins_with("Address", '7834 24th')

examples.label: SELECT using contains function
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Orders" WHERE "OrderID"=1 AND contains("Address", 'Kirkland')

examples.label: SELECT using IS MISSING
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM Music WHERE "Awards" is MISSING

examples.label: EXISTS condition check with IS MISSING
examples.sql: EXISTS(SELECT * FROM "Music" where Artist='No One You Know' and SongTitle='Call Me Today' and Awards is  MISSING)

examples.label: Parameterized INSERT (single param)
examples.sql: INSERT INTO Music value {'Artist':?,'SongTitle':'?'}

examples.label: UPDATE inside transaction (quoted table)
examples.sql: UPDATE "Music" SET AwardsWon=1 SET AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]}  where Artist='Acme Band' and SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: SELECT (item exists, all attributes)
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Music" WHERE Artist='No One You Know' and SongTitle='Call Me Today'

examples.label: SELECT non-existent projected attribute
examples.sql: SELECT non_existent_projected_attribute FROM "Music" WHERE Artist='No One You Know' and SongTitle='Call Me Today'

examples.label: SELECT non-existent item
examples.sql: SELECT * FROM "Music" WHERE Artist='No One I Know' and SongTitle='Call You Today'

examples.label: Batch SELECT first item
examples.sql: SELECT pk FROM ProblemSet WHERE pk = 'p#9StkWHYTxm7x2AqSXcrfu7' AND sk = 'info'

examples.label: Batch SELECT second item
examples.sql: SELECT pk FROM ProblemSet WHERE pk = 'p#isC2ChceGbxHgESc4szoTE' AND sk = 'info'

examples.label: Batch parameterized INSERT
examples.sql: INSERT INTO Music VALUE {'Artist':?,'SongTitle':?}

examples.label: Batch UPDATE with comma-separated SET
examples.sql: UPDATE Music SET AwardsWon=1, AwardDetail={'Grammys':[2020, 2018]} WHERE Artist='Acme Band' AND SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'

examples.label: Batch INSERT (Java SDK example) with literal tuple
examples.sql: INSERT INTO Music value {'Artist':'Acme Band','SongTitle':'PartiQL Rocks'}

examples.label: Batch UPDATE (Java SDK example) lowercase keywords
examples.sql: UPDATE Music set AwardDetail.BillBoard=[2020] where Artist='Acme Band' and SongTitle='PartiQL Rocks'
